name: New York Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to New York - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: State Board for Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: New York State Education Department (NYSED), Office of the Professions

address.description: New York State Education Department, Office of the Professions, Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Unit, 89 Washington Avenue, Albany, NY 12234-1000

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts (Form 2)
requiredDocuments.description: Certification of Professional Education from a CAA-accredited program. May incur an additional institutional fee.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Sent directly to NYSED using agency code R7747.

requiredDocuments.name: Supervised experience forms (4A/4B)
requiredDocuments.description: Verification of 36 weeks of supervised professional experience signed by supervisor.

requiredDocuments.name: Child abuse and harassment training
requiredDocuments.description: Completion certificates for mandated New York coursework in child abuse identification and harassment prevention.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a New York SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial licensure and registration fee is $294, submitted with Form 1 to NYSED. A Limited License for supervised practice costs an additional $70. Triennial renewal is $155.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a TSSLD to work in New York schools?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. To work as an SLP in a New York school setting, you need the Teacher of Students with Speech and Language Disability (TSSLD) credential in addition to your state SLP license. The TSSLD is issued by NYSED and has separate application requirements.

mainEntity.name: How long does the New York SLP application take?

acceptedAnswer.text: Processing time is approximately six to eight weeks once all documents are received by NYSED. Incomplete applications will cause significant delays. Submit all forms and fees together to avoid processing issues.

mainEntity.name: Does New York have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in New York. To work in New York, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to New York?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 6 to 8 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in New York while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
